stage.subtitle_ar: المرحلة الأخيرة: رسائل عمل تفتح الأبواب، ولغة تفاوض متقدّمة، وعرض تقديمي يليق بعملك أمام أي جهة.

content.intro_ar: في عالم الأعمال اليوم، رسالة بريد واحدة مكتوبة بإتقان قد تفتح لك بابًا مع مورّد جديد أو عميل كبير. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم كيف تكتب رسالة عمل احترافية بالإنجليزية: من سطر الموضوع إلى الخاتمة المهذّبة. تخيّل مورّدًا تريد التعامل معه، وستجد أن كل عبارة هنا قابلة للاستخدام غدًا صباحًا.

vocab.meaning_en: the title of an email
vocab.example: Write a clear subject line before you send the email.

vocab.meaning_en: the person who receives a message
vocab.example: Check the recipient's address before sending.

vocab.meaning_en: a file sent with an email
vocab.example: The price list is in the attachment.

vocab.meaning_en: official and polite in style
vocab.example: A formal email starts with 'Dear Mr. Ahmed'.

vocab.meaning_en: a question asking for information
vocab.example: I sent an inquiry about the new filters.

vocab.meaning_en: an answer to a message
vocab.example: I received a reply from the supplier today.

vocab.meaning_en: to say that something is certain
vocab.example: Please confirm the order before Thursday.

vocab.meaning_en: to send a received message to someone else
vocab.example: Forward the invoice to the accountant.

vocab.meaning_en: the last time you can do something
vocab.example: The deadline for payment is Sunday.

vocab.meaning_en: a polite way to end an email
vocab.example: He ended the email with 'Best regards'.

vocab.meaning_en: showing good manners
vocab.example: A polite email gets a faster answer.

paragraphs: Last month, Dhafer needed a new supplier for brake pads. Instead of calling, he decided to send a formal email. He knew that a clear, polite email often gets a better answer than a long phone call, because the recipient can read it at a good time and reply with exact numbers.

His subject line was short and clear: 'Inquiry – Brake Pads for Toyota and Hyundai'. In the first line, he introduced his workshop in the industrial district. Then he asked three direct questions about price, quality, and delivery time. He added his current price list as an attachment, so the supplier could see what he already pays.

The reply came the next morning. The supplier offered a good price and asked Dhafer to confirm his order before Thursday, because the deadline for the next shipment was close. Dhafer wrote back, confirmed the quantity, and asked the supplier to forward the invoice to his accountant.

He ended every email the same way: a short thank you, then 'Best regards' and his full name. Six months later, this supplier gives him the best prices in the market. One polite, formal email — about 3,000 riyals saved every month.

key_phrases.en: I am writing to inquire about your prices for brake pads.
key_phrases.ar: أكتب إليكم للاستفسار عن أسعاركم لفحمات الفرامل.

key_phrases.en: Please find the price list attached.
Could you confirm the delivery date, please?
key_phrases.ar: هل يمكنكم تأكيد موعد التسليم، من فضلكم؟

key_phrases.en: Thank you for your quick reply.
I look forward to hearing from you.

key_phrases.ar: مع أطيب التحيات، ظافر آل قهيدان

comprehension.question: Why did Dhafer choose email instead of a phone call?
options: The recipient can read it at a good time and reply with exact numbers
comprehension.correct: The recipient can read it at a good time and reply with exact numbers
comprehension.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن الرسالة الواضحة المهذّبة تصل غالبًا إلى ردّ أفضل، لأن المستلم يقرؤها في وقت مناسب ويردّ بأرقام دقيقة.

comprehension.question: What did Dhafer add as an attachment?
options: A map of the industrial district
comprehension.explanation_ar: أرفق ظافر قائمة الأسعار الحالية ليطّلع المورّد على ما يدفعه الآن.

comprehension.question: What did the supplier ask Dhafer to do before Thursday?
comprehension.explanation_ar: طلب المورّد تأكيد الطلبية قبل يوم الخميس لأن الموعد النهائي للشحنة القادمة كان قريبًا.

comprehension.question: Who received the forwarded invoice?
comprehension.explanation_ar: طلب ظافر من المورّد إعادة إرسال الفاتورة إلى محاسبه.

comprehension.question: How does Dhafer end every email?
options: With a thank you and 'Best regards'
comprehension.correct: With a thank you and 'Best regards'
comprehension.explanation_ar: يختم ظافر كل رسالة بشكر قصير ثم عبارة 'Best regards' واسمه الكامل — وهذه خاتمة العمل المعتادة.

task.prompt_en: Write a short formal email (4–6 sentences) to a parts supplier: introduce your business, ask about the price of one product, and end with a polite closing.
task.prompt_ar: اكتب رسالة عمل قصيرة (٤–٦ جُمل) إلى مورّد قطع غيار: عرّف بعملك، واسأل عن سعر منتج واحد، واختم بخاتمة مهذّبة.
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل subject line وattachment وconfirm وregards.

content.intro_ar: أنت تتفاوض كل يوم: مع مورّد، مع عميل، مع صاحب عقار. في هذا الدرس سترتقي بلغة التفاوض عندك إلى المستوى الاحترافي: العرض المضادّ، والتنازل المحسوب، والحدّ الأدنى الذي لا تكشفه مبكرًا. هذه العبارات صُمّمت لتخدمك في صفقة حقيقية، لا في كتاب.

vocab.meaning_en: a discussion to reach an agreement
vocab.example: The negotiation with the supplier took ninety minutes.

vocab.meaning_en: a new offer made in response to another offer
vocab.example: He rejected their price and made a counter-offer.

vocab.meaning_en: something you give up to reach an agreement
vocab.example: Free delivery was a big concession from the supplier.

vocab.meaning_en: an agreement where each side gives something
vocab.example: A fair compromise protects the relationship.

vocab.meaning_en: the conditions of an agreement
vocab.example: We improved the payment terms to sixty days.

vocab.meaning_en: power that helps you get what you want
vocab.example: Being a big customer gives you leverage.

vocab.meaning_en: the minimum you can accept
vocab.example: Never show your bottom line too early.

vocab.meaning_en: good for both sides
vocab.example: We reached a win-win agreement in the end.

vocab.meaning_en: ready to change or adapt
vocab.example: Be flexible on small points, not on the big numbers.

vocab.meaning_en: a promise to do something
vocab.example: He signed a commitment to buy oil for a full year.

vocab.meaning_en: continuing far into the future
vocab.example: A long-term contract protects both businesses.

paragraphs: Every year, Dhafer sits down with his main parts supplier to negotiate the annual contract. It is the most important negotiation in his calendar: filters, oil, and brake pads for twelve months. A one-riyal difference on a filter, multiplied by thousands of pieces, decides whether his year is comfortable or tight.

This year, the supplier opened with a five percent price increase. Dhafer did not argue immediately. He knew his leverage: his workshop had become one of the supplier's biggest customers in the industrial district. So he made a counter-offer — he would accept a two percent increase if the supplier improved the payment terms from thirty to sixty days.

The supplier hesitated, so Dhafer offered a concession of his own: a written commitment to buy oil exclusively from him for the whole year. In return, he asked for free delivery — his bottom line. Both sides gave something, both sides gained something. That is the meaning of a genuine compromise: not a victory for one side, but a win-win agreement that protects a long-term relationship.

They signed after ninety minutes. Dhafer's rule is simple: stay flexible on small points, stay firm on the numbers that really matter, and never let the other side guess your bottom line too early. A negotiation is not a battle — it is a conversation about the future of two businesses.

key_phrases.en: I'm afraid we can't accept those terms as they stand.
key_phrases.ar: أخشى أننا لا نستطيع قبول هذه الشروط كما هي.
اسمح لي أن أقدّم عرضًا مضادًّا.

key_phrases.en: If you improve the payment terms, we can commit to a larger order.
key_phrases.ar: إذا حسّنتم شروط الدفع، يمكننا الالتزام بطلبية أكبر.

key_phrases.en: We are flexible on delivery, but firm on price.
key_phrases.ar: نحن مرنون في موعد التسليم، لكننا ثابتون في السعر.

key_phrases.en: We are looking for a win-win agreement.
key_phrases.ar: نبحث عن اتفاق يكسب فيه الطرفان.

comprehension.question: What did the supplier ask for at the start of the negotiation?
comprehension.explanation_ar: افتتح المورّد التفاوض بطلب زيادة في الأسعار قدرها خمسة بالمئة.

comprehension.question: What was Dhafer's counter-offer?
options: A two percent increase in exchange for sixty-day payment terms
A bigger order of brake pads
comprehension.correct: A two percent increase in exchange for sixty-day payment terms
comprehension.explanation_ar: قبل ظافر زيادة اثنين بالمئة فقط مقابل تحسين شروط الدفع من ثلاثين إلى ستين يومًا — هذا هو العرض المضادّ.

comprehension.question: What concession did Dhafer offer?
options: Buying oil exclusively from the supplier for a year
comprehension.correct: Buying oil exclusively from the supplier for a year
comprehension.explanation_ar: قدّم ظافر تنازلًا من طرفه: التزام مكتوب بشراء الزيت حصريًا من هذا المورّد لمدة سنة كاملة.

comprehension.question: What was Dhafer's bottom line in this negotiation?
comprehension.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن التوصيل المجاني كان حدّه الأدنى المقبول — الشرط الذي لا يتنازل عنه.

comprehension.question: According to the reading, what is a negotiation?
options: A conversation about the future of two businesses
A way to trick the other side
comprehension.correct: A conversation about the future of two businesses
comprehension.explanation_ar: يختم النص بأن التفاوض ليس معركة، بل حوار حول مستقبل عملَين — علاقة طويلة الأمد لا انتصار لحظي.

task.prompt_en: Imagine your supplier wants to raise prices by five percent. Speak for one minute: make a counter-offer, and explain what you can give and what you need in return.
task.prompt_ar: تخيّل أن مورّدك يريد رفع الأسعار خمسة بالمئة. تحدّث لمدة دقيقة: قدّم عرضًا مضادًّا، ووضّح ما يمكنك تقديمه وما تحتاجه في المقابل.
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل counter-offer وterms وflexible وwin-win.

content.intro_ar: قد يأتي يوم تقف فيه أمام بنك أو مستثمر أو شريك محتمل لتعرض عملك في دقائق معدودة. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم كيف تبني عرضًا تقديميًا واضحًا وواثقًا بالإنجليزية: نظرة عامة، وأرقام تُبرز إنجازاتك، وخاتمة تفتح باب الأسئلة. حضّر قصتك جيدًا — فأنت أعرف الناس بورشتك.

vocab.meaning_en: a talk that shows information to people
vocab.example: His presentation to the bank lasted five minutes.

vocab.meaning_en: a short general description
vocab.example: Start with an overview of your business.

vocab.meaning_en: the people listening to you
vocab.example: Look at your audience while you speak.

vocab.meaning_en: one page of a presentation
vocab.example: Put only one idea on each slide.

vocab.meaning_en: to make something easy to notice
vocab.example: Highlight your best numbers early in the talk.

vocab.meaning_en: something good that you have done
vocab.example: Growing for three years in a row is a real achievement.

vocab.meaning_en: the money a business receives
vocab.example: Our revenue grew by twenty percent this year.

vocab.meaning_en: an increase in size or amount
vocab.example: The plan shows strong growth for next year.

vocab.meaning_en: what makes your business better than others
vocab.example: Fast, honest service is our competitive advantage.

vocab.meaning_en: to grow bigger
vocab.example: We plan to expand to a second branch.

vocab.meaning_en: sure of yourself
vocab.example: Speak in a calm, confident voice.

vocab.meaning_en: a short statement of the main points
vocab.example: End with a clear summary of the plan.

reading.title_en: Five Minutes That Changed the Workshop
paragraphs: When Dhafer decided to open a second branch, the bank asked him to present his business plan in person. He had run a successful workshop for fifteen years, yet standing in front of an audience with a laptop and slides felt harder than rebuilding an engine. So he prepared the presentation the way he prepares a car: piece by piece.

He started with a short overview: who he is, what the workshop does, and who his customers are. Then he used numbers to highlight his strongest achievements — revenue had grown by twenty percent for three years in a row, and seven out of ten customers came back. His competitive advantage was easy to explain: honest prices, fast service, and a name people trust in the neighborhood.

Near the end, he explained why he wanted to expand: the industrial district was full, demand kept rising, and his team was ready. He finished with a confident summary of the plan — the cost, the expected growth, and the date the new branch would open. Then he smiled and said, 'I would be happy to take your questions.'

The bank approved the loan within a week. Later, the manager told him the secret was not the slides — it was the clarity. A good presentation does not try to say everything; it makes the audience remember three things and believe the person saying them.

key_phrases.en: Thank you all for coming. Let me give you a quick overview of our business.
key_phrases.ar: شكرًا لحضوركم جميعًا. اسمحوا لي أن أقدّم نظرة عامة سريعة عن عملنا.

key_phrases.en: Our revenue has grown by twenty percent this year.
key_phrases.ar: نمت إيراداتنا بنسبة عشرين بالمئة هذا العام.

key_phrases.en: Our main competitive advantage is fast, honest service.
key_phrases.ar: ميزتنا التنافسية الأساسية هي الخدمة السريعة والصادقة.

key_phrases.en: I would like to highlight three achievements.
To summarize, we are ready to expand.
I would be happy to take your questions.

comprehension.question: What did the bank ask Dhafer to do?
options: Present his business plan in person
comprehension.correct: Present his business plan in person
comprehension.explanation_ar: طلب البنك من ظافر أن يقدّم خطة عمله بنفسه قبل الموافقة على تمويل الفرع الثاني.

comprehension.question: How did Dhafer prove his workshop was strong?
options: He used numbers, like twenty percent revenue growth
He brought his customers to the bank
He promised to work more hours
comprehension.correct: He used numbers, like twenty percent revenue growth
comprehension.explanation_ar: استخدم ظافر الأرقام ليُبرز إنجازاته: نموّ الإيرادات عشرين بالمئة لثلاث سنوات متتالية، وعودة سبعة من كل عشرة عملاء.

comprehension.question: What was his competitive advantage?
options: The cheapest parts in the city
Honest prices, fast service, and a trusted name
comprehension.correct: Honest prices, fast service, and a trusted name
comprehension.explanation_ar: لخّص النص ميزته التنافسية في ثلاثة أشياء: أسعار صادقة، وخدمة سريعة، واسم يثق به الناس في الحي.

comprehension.question: What did Dhafer say at the end of his presentation?
options: 'I would be happy to take your questions'
comprehension.correct: 'I would be happy to take your questions'
comprehension.explanation_ar: ختم ظافر عرضه بابتسامة وعبارة فتح باب الأسئلة — وهي الخاتمة الاحترافية لأي عرض تقديمي.

comprehension.question: According to the bank manager, what made the presentation successful?
comprehension.explanation_ar: قال مدير البنك إن السرّ لم يكن في الشرائح بل في الوضوح: عرض جيد يجعل الحضور يتذكّرون ثلاثة أشياء ويصدّقون قائلها.

task.prompt_en: Present your business in five to six sentences: give an overview, mention one achievement with a number, state your competitive advantage, and explain why you want to grow.
task.prompt_ar: قدّم عرضًا عن عملك في خمس إلى ست جُمل: نظرة عامة، وإنجاز واحد برقم، وميزتك التنافسية، وسبب رغبتك في النموّ.
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، وتحدّث بصوت هادئ وواثق كأنك أمام البنك فعلًا.
